generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller interfacing with Observable Manufacturing Element via Data Collection and Device Control Entity following ISO 23247 Part 2 Reference Architecture"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor deployed for spatial mapping and obstacle detection in 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution 3D imaging camera used for environmental perception in autonomous vehicle system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelDepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system capturing vehicle dynamics including position, velocity, and ESC data for ACC validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor actuation signals and steering servo inputs for vehicle control system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationControlSignals
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance IntelDepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationControlSignals : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional Entity executing LiDAR Clustering & Tracking to convert raw point clouds into structured object tracks and driving path projections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingFunctionalEntity
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and behavioral model implementing ACC logic for adaptive speed regulation and collision avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance PointCloudTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and driving path projections derived from raw LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFunctionalEntity
]

instance StandardizedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data converted to standardized units for visualization and control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance ProcessedActuationSignals : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor actuation and steering servo inputs serialized and aligned for control system integration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationControlSignals
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity performing data quality assurance, sensor input validation, and coordinate system alignment"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityAssuranceService, PathProjectionService
]

instance DataTranslationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for all data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStandardizationService
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core Digital Twin Entity implementing digital representation of Observable Manufacturing Element with geometric, behavioral, and analytical models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSimulationService, ControlValidationService
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 framework enabling real-time data acquisition, processing, and control command execution between physical and digital domains"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataAcquisitionService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency embedded platform running ROS2 nodes and real-time simulations for edge processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables EdgeProcessingService
]

instance SQLDatabaseStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured storage system for telemetry data including sensor readings and control commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPersistenceService
]

instance DataPersistenceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service storing telemetry data including sensor readings and control commands"
]

// C6: Services
instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating sensor inputs and ensuring data consistency with Digital Twin coordinate system"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

instance PathProjectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service projecting driving paths from LiDAR data for trajectory planning and collision avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:provides PathVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataStandardizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service standardizing data formats and units across all sensor streams for interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:provides StandardizedDataInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously streaming sensor feeds (LiDAR, cameras, vehicle dynamics) to Digital Twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance RealTimeSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing high-fidelity ACC logic validation using real-world sensor data and digital models"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

instance ControlValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating control commands through hybrid simulation of physical and digital models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlEffectivenessInsight
]

instance EdgeProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offloading high-fidelity simulation workloads to edge servers for reduced latency"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote configuration and troubleshooting of ADAS parameters through Digital Twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteConfigurationAction
]

instance ParameterTuningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating optimization of ACC gain parameters using Digital Twin simulations"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationInsight
]

instance ScenarioReplayService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service reconstructing and analyzing critical incidents captured by Digital Twin for post-mortem analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides IncidentAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight verifying consistency between sensor inputs and Digital Twin coordinate system alignment"
]

instance PathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight visualizing projected driving paths for trajectory planning and collision avoidance"
]

instance StandardizedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight confirming all data streams are standardized for interoperability across system components"
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing continuous visualization of vehicle state data and sensor feeds"
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating ACC logic through hybrid simulation of real-world sensor data and digital models"
]

instance ControlEffectivenessInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight assessing effectiveness of control commands through digital twin validation"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying performance bottlenecks and optimization opportunities in edge processing"
]

instance ParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight presenting optimized ACC gain parameters for collision risk minimization"
]

instance IncidentAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight analyzing critical incidents through reconstructed scenario replays"
]

instance RemoteConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling remote configuration and troubleshooting of ADAS parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action triggering emergency braking based on collision risk assessment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action adjusting vehicle speed according to ACC parameters and traffic conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environments/Components
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical twin comprising 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with LiDAR, cameras, and ESC integration for road traffic simulation"
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation implementing geometric, behavioral, and analytical models for ACC validation"
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface component providing monitoring dashboards and control panels for system operators"
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

// C13: Entity Relationships
instance ObservableManufacturingElement : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Observable Manufacturing Element representing the physical autonomous vehicle system"
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance DataCollectionDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Entity aggregating sensor data and controlling physical acting components"
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VedderElectronicSpeedController
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity implementing digital representation with modeling capabilities"
    base:contains LiDARClusteringTrackingModel, AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, PointCloudTrackingData, StandardizedVehicleStateData, ProcessedActuationSignals
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "User Entity providing interfaces for monitoring and control"
    base:contains RemoteManagementService, ScenarioReplayService
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DTS is facilitated by the **Controlling FE (ACC)** and **Actuation FE**, enabling closed-loop control of the physical vehicle. Key components include:
- **Command Types**:
  - **Speed adjustments** (e.g., adaptive cruise control setpoints).
  - **Emergency braking** (hard deceleration commands).
  - **Steering corrections** (via servo actuation FE).
  - **Path deviation alerts** (projected driving path adjustments based on object tracking).

- **Control Message Formats**:
  - Commands are serialized and transmitted via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures message alignment with the **Actuation FE** (motor/servo interfaces).
  - Messages include **unit-converted values** (e.g., normalized steering angles, velocity in m/s).

- **Validation and Authentication**:
  - **No explicit authentication mechanisms** are documented for control signals (e.g., no encryption or digital signatures mentioned).
  - **Feedback systems** rely on **real-time vehicle state data** (e.g., LiDAR object tracks, motor responses) to validate command execution.

- **Conflict Resolution**:
  - The system implements **performance class I ACC**, which lacks **road curvature capabilities**, limiting adaptive control to straight-path scenarios.
  - **No explicit conflict resolution policies** (e.g., priority-based execution or arbitration) are described for concurrent commands (e.g., braking vs. steering).

- **Command Execution Confirmation**:
  - Confirmation is implicit via **closed-loop feedback**: the **Actuation FE** receives acknowledgment from the physical actuators (motor/servo responses), which are then reflected in the **vehicle state data** fed back to the Digital Twin.
  - **No explicit error recovery protocols** (e.g., retries or fallback commands) are documented for failed executions.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to stricter vehicular application standards despite hardware limitations. Temporal granularity is dictated by the ROS2-based execution model, where each Function Entity (FE) operates as an independent ROS2 package with nodes implementing specific functionalities. Latency requirements are implicitly governed by the real-time synchronization constraints of the Physical Twin Entity (e.g., sensor data processing, actuation, and device communication), which must align with the 100 ms clock synchronization to maintain consistency between the physical and digital domains. No explicit mention of service-specific time scales or granularity beyond the 100 ms constraint is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts an **entity-based architectural pattern** inspired by ISO 23247’s Reference Architecture (RA), with adaptations to accommodate vehicular-specific requirements. The system is orchestrated via **ROS2 packages**, where each package encapsulates one or more ROS2 nodes implementing a Function Entity (FE). Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **ROS2-based inter-node communication** for intra-entity and inter-entity synchronization.
  - **Hardware-specific deployment** of entities (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) to optimize resource ownership and engineering processes.
  - **Deviations from ISO 23247** to align with the system’s resource-centric design (e.g., merging OME-like functionalities into the Physical Twin Entity).
  Resource management is implicit within the entity boundaries, with the *Physical Twin Entity* hosting general automotive functionalities (e.g., sensor processing) and the *Digital Twin Entity* managing data workflows. The system lacks explicit mention of high-level orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, edge orchestration) but relies on ROS2’s distributed execution model for system-wide coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard defines a **development framework for Digital Twins (DTs)** in manufacturing, structured into four parts, with **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)** being the most relevant for lifecycle considerations. The architecture is **entity-based**, comprising four primary entities: the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)** (physical assets like equipment, facilities, or processes), the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** (responsible for bidirectional data acquisition and control), the **Digital Twin Entity** (digital representation of the OME), and the **User Entity** (interface for human interaction). Each entity is decomposed into **Functional Entities (FEs)**, modular units handling specific tasks such as actuation, data transformation, or user interfacing. While the standard does not explicitly enumerate traditional lifecycle phases (e.g., design, deployment, operation, retirement), it implies lifecycle management through **modular adaptability** of FEs and entities. **Part 3 (Digital Representation)** specifies requirements for digital modeling of physical elements, ensuring consistency in representation across lifecycle stages, while **Part 4 (Information Exchange)** standardizes data formats and protocols (e.g., OPC UA) for interoperability. Transition management between stages is **implicitly supported** via the entity-based modularity, allowing dynamic reconfiguration of FEs without redesigning the entire DT. Governance is implied through adherence to the standard’s **entity-based framework**, ensuring traceability and alignment with manufacturing processes. However, explicit lifecycle governance approaches (e.g., versioning, compliance tracking) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The documents implicitly address **fidelity and validity** through:
- **Real-Time Requirements**: The system is designed as a 'real-time' Digital Twin (per the Introduction), ensuring low-latency reflections of physical OMEs.
- **Latency Constraints**: Requirement **R7** mandates a **network latency of at most 100 ms** to maintain synchronization between physical and digital representations.
- **Clock Synchronization**: Requirement **R8** emphasizes **high-precision clock synchronization** (though exact accuracy thresholds are not specified) to ensure temporal validity of data exchanges.
No explicit methods for **validation, verification, uncertainty quantification, or error correction** are described in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of the DTS is designed for low-latency, real-time communication between entities, with the following specifications:
- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **Intra-entity communication** (within Digital Twin Entity) uses **ROS2**, a middleware framework for robotics, enabling publish-subscribe messaging with QoS policies (though specific QoS configurations are not detailed).
  - **Inter-entity communication** (Physical Twin ↔ Digital Twin) occurs over **Wi-Fi** using **UDP** (transport layer), optimized for low-latency data transfer.

- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  - **Distributed architecture**: Each entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User Entity) is deployed on **separate hardware components**, connected via a centralized Wi-Fi network.
  - **No explicit mention of a central gateway or edge server** for protocol translation or aggregation.

- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  - **Latency constraint**: The DTS shall maintain **network latency ≤ 100 ms** from the vehicle (Physical Twin) to the Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Frequency requirements**: Not explicitly quantified, but implied to support **real-time visualization** (e.g., 30+ Hz for LiDAR point clouds, though this is speculative based on typical automotive LiDAR systems).

- **Reliability and Redundancy**:
  - **No redundancy mechanisms** (e.g., failover nodes, dual Wi-Fi links) are documented.
  - **No explicit QoS policies** (e.g., priority-based traffic shaping) for critical data streams (e.g., emergency braking commands).

- **Security Protocols**:
  - **No security protocols** (e.g., TLS, IPsec, or authentication) are mentioned for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. **Hosting platforms** are not specified (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), nor are **computational/storage requirements** (e.g., CPU/GPU specs, RAM, or storage capacity). **Scalability mechanisms** are implied through the modular **Functional Entity (FE)** architecture (e.g., Data Pre-Processing, Reporting, Controlling), but deployment models (e.g., containerization, microservices) are not described. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not mentioned, though the use of **ROS2** and **Wi-Fi/UDP** suggests potential redundancy could be implemented. **Deployment models supported** are not clarified, but the system’s reliance on **ROS2** and **Wi-Fi** indicates a **distributed deployment** with potential for **edge computing** (e.g., onboard vehicle processing). **Not in Document** for precise details on hosting, scalability, or redundancy strategies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The **standardization compliance** of the Digital Twin (DT) system is governed primarily by the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing**, with additional implications for interoperability and industry-specific requirements. Key aspects include:

**Relevant Industry Standards:**
1. **ISO 23247 (2021)**:
   - **Scope**: Defines a **development framework** for DTs in manufacturing, organized into four parts:
     - **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**: Establishes an **entity-based RA** with four primary roles:
       - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Physical assets (e.g., equipment, processes).
       - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Handles **data acquisition** and **device actuation**.
       - **Digital Twin Entity**: Provides the **digital representation** of the OME.
       - **User Entity**: Offers **interfaces** for user interaction.
     - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Modular components within each entity (e.g., data transformation, user visualization).
   - **Interoperability**: While not prescriptive, the standard implies adherence to **industry-wide protocols** (e.g., OPC UA) for seamless communication between entities.

2. **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**:
   - **Mentioned as a Potential Protocol**: The standard suggests OPC UA as a **standardized communication framework** for DT systems, enabling **secure, platform-independent data exchange** between entities.
   - **Not Mandatory**: The standard does not enforce OPC UA but positions it as a **best practice** for interoperability.

**Specifications and Certification Requirements:**
- **No Explicit Certification Framework**: ISO 23247 does not outline **certification bodies** or **compliance validation processes**, but adherence to the standard is implied as a **de facto governance mechanism**.
- **Modular Compliance**: The entity-based RA allows **incremental compliance** with standards, as each entity (e.g., Data Collection, Digital Twin) can be validated independently.

**Compliance Frameworks:**
1. **ISO 23247 Alignment**:
   - **Development Governance**: The standard provides a **structural framework** for DT development, ensuring **consistency** in entity design and functionality.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: The modular architecture supports **phased compliance** as the DT evolves (e.g., adding new FEs or entities).
2. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - **OPC UA**: If adopted, ensures **cross-vendor compatibility** and **data consistency** across the DT system.
   - **No Other Standards Mentioned**: The provided documents do not reference additional frameworks (e.g., IEC 62264 for manufacturing execution systems).

**Interoperability Standards Adopted:**
- **OPC UA**: The only explicitly mentioned standard for **interoperability**, enabling:
  - **Real-time data synchronization** between the OME and Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Secure communication** across heterogeneous systems (e.g., PLCs, ERP).
  - **Plug-and-play integration** of new FEs or entities.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** and **ISO 26262-1:2018** standards to ensure functional safety and reliability. Key considerations include:
- **Safety-critical system compliance**: The system must respect the safety-critical nature of ACC, as referenced in **Section III.A** of the context, ensuring no degradation of safety performance under operational conditions.
- **Latency requirements**: Derived from **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)**, the system must meet **low-latency constraints** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services, particularly for **safety-critical communications** (Requirement **R7**).
- **Clock synchronization**: The system must incorporate **precise time synchronization** (Requirement **R8**) to ensure accurate timing for safety-critical operations, aligning with **ISO 15622** performance requirements.
- **Fail-safe mechanisms**: The Digital Twin must incorporate **safety mechanisms** to mitigate risks associated with ACC system failures, as implied by **ISO 26262-1:2018** for road vehicle functional safety.
- **Risk assessment**: Safety-related risks must be evaluated using **ISO 26262-1** guidelines, including **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) decomposition** to classify and mitigate hazards.
- **No explicit mention of**: Role-based access control (RBAC), cryptographic hashing (e.g., SHA-256), or detailed threat mitigation strategies beyond the referenced standards.
"
]
